channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLtech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Apple's next chapter, SpaceX and Cursor strike a deal, Palantir's manifesto
item.description: Tim Cook's legacy and future of Apple discussed on Uncanny Valley podcast.

item.title: Palantir employees wonder if they're the bad guys
item.description: Internal Slack messages reveal workforce in turmoil, WIRED reports.

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford students learn from Silicon Valley royalty
item.description: CS 153 goes viral on campus and X, but not everyone is happy.

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, moving closer to AI 'super app'
item.description: New model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Bot reads the internet so you don't have to.

item.title: New smart glasses from ex-OnePlus engineers have a hidden cost
item.description: Kickstarter glasses include AI features for one year; future pricing unknown.

item.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs, or 8,000 employees, report says
item.description: Internal memo indicates sweeping layoffs to begin May 20.

item.title: Don't stop hiring humans — stop hiring wrong humans, Artisan founder says
item.description: Every founder needs to assemble the right team to scale.

item.title: Lume Cube Edge Light Go review: versatile, portable
item.description: Powerful, precise, and multipurpose portable lamp impresses.

item.title: Trump's pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
item.description: Sean Plankey withdraws after a year of chaotic temporary leadership.
